description.p: Morrigan Xanderreef was a revered Speaker of Spirits within Cnidaria and necromancer, known for his mastery of life and death and his ruthless political strategies.

A master of necromancy, Morrigan possessed a profound understanding of life, death, and arcane manipulation. His experiments were precise, calculated, and deeply scholarly - yet driven by a desire to rival the gods themselves.
Despite his intellect, Morrigan was flamboyant, arrogant, and indulgent. He reveled in attention, weaving grand narratives about his power and destiny while manipulating those around him.
Warned of a coming cataclysm, Morrigan chose not salvation - but escape. Through lichdom, he abandoned responsibility and embraced eternal freedom.
Now known as the Sovereign of the Maelstrom, Morrigan exists in decadent unlife, pursuing power, chaos, and domination over death itself.
Your vedalken lich character has the following racial traits.
Ability Score Increase. Your Intelligence score increases by 4, your Wisdom score increases by 2, and your Charisma score increases by 4.
Size. Your size is Medium.
Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
Four Armed. You have four slender arms that can hold and manipulate objects, or be used to cast spells. Only two may be used to wield weapons or shields at any time.
Perfect Undeath. Your form is sustained entirely by necromantic will. You are immune to effects that would reduce your maximum hit points, age you, or magically alter your form against your will.
Lich Physiology. You are an undead creature. You possess extensive damage and condition immunities, and are resistant to effects that would alter your body or mind against your will.
Lich's Willpower. Your maximum hit points can't be reduced by any means.
Legendary Actions. Immediately after another creature's turn, the lich can expend a use to take a special action such as teleporting, disrupting life, or unleashing a frightening gaze.
Speaker's Corruption. As an action, you may charm summoned creatures within 120 feet.
Spirit Jar. If destroyed, you reform in 1d10 days as long as your phylactery exists.
Legendary Resistance. If you fail a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead a limited number of times per day.
Fear Aura. Creatures within 60 feet may become frightened.
Paralyzing Touch. Your touch carries freezing necromantic power that can leave foes paralyzed.
You have four slender arms that can hold and manipulate objects, or be used to cast spells.
Only two may be used to wield weapons or shields at any time.

sheet.description: Four arms; only two usable for weapons or shields.

description.p: Your form is sustained entirely by necromantic will.
You are immune to effects that would reduce your maximum hit points, age you, or magically alter your form against your will.

sheet.description: Immune to max HP reduction, aging, and forced transformation.

description.p: You are an undead creature.
Damage Immunities: Cold, Lightning, Necrotic, Poison.
Condition Immunities: Charmed, Exhaustion, Frightened, Paralyzed, Poisoned.
Additional Immunities (3E Lich): You are immune to polymorph effects, unless you willingly accept effects you cast on yourself, and are immune to mind-affecting effects.

sheet.description: Undead. Immune to cold, lightning, necrotic, poison, charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, polymorph, and mind-affecting effects.

description.p: Your maximum hit points can't be reduced by any means.

sheet.description: Your maximum hit points can't be reduced.

description.p: Legendary Action Uses: 3 (4 in Lair).
Immediately after another creature's turn, the lich can expend a use to take one of the following actions.
Deathly Teleport. Teleport 60 feet; nearby creatures take necrotic damage.
Disrupt Life. Deal area necrotic damage.
Frightening Gaze. Cast fear.
As an action, charm all summoned creatures within 120 feet on a failed DC 18 Wisdom saving throw for 1 minute.
If destroyed, you reform in 1d10 days as long as your phylactery exists.
You regain all hit points upon reforming.

sheet.description: Reform in 1d10 days if phylactery survives.

description.p: 4/day (5 in lair), if you fail a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead.
Creatures within 60 feet must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become frightened.
Melee Spell Attack: 3d6 cold damage and the target may become paralyzed.
